sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'W', 'B', 'c']:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        maxr = max(p[0] for p in b)
        maxc = max(p[1] for p in b)
        if n['pixels'] <= 20:
            print(f"id={n['id']:2d} color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']:3d} [{minr},{minc}]-[{maxr},{maxc}] children={n['children']}")

# Check the ASCII around the tokens
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(29, 34):
    row_str = ascii_lines[i][15:35]
    row_str = row_str.replace('g', '·')
    print(f"R{i}: {row_str}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After LEFT, the token went back to its position at [30,21]-[32,23] with the white center at [31,22]. The token is back to normal (8 green pixels + 1 white center = 9 pixels total, same as the other token). The children list shows id=6 has children=[10] which is the white pixel.
So LEFT reversed the RIGHT movement. The movement is reversible. Now I understand:
- Arrow keys move the left green token (the one with white center)
- RIGHT moves it right by 3 cells
- LEFT moves it left by 3 cells
- The token can move horizontally on row 30-32
Now I need to figure out:
1. How to move the other token (with black center)
2. How to move tokens vertically
3. What the goal is (probably to put tokens into containers)
Let me try UP and DOWN to see if the token can move vertically. Also, let me try to figure out how to switch which token is controlled.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the initial state. Looking at the original layout:
- Left platform: container at [32,11]-[36,15], token at [30,18]-[32,20]
- Middle: token at [30,27]-[32,29], white square at [30,30]-[32,32]
- Purple wall at [21,33]-[41,38]
- Right platform: container at [26,44]-[30,48]
The left container is at rows 32-36, and the right container is at rows 26-30. The tokens are at rows 30-32. So to put a token into a container, it needs to be at the right position.
Let me try UP to see if the token moves up.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try UP to see vertical movement
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
